headlineSummary: OpenAI drew the clearest negative reaction, driven by criticism of sycophancy and “lobotomized” models, while Anthropic had high-volume mixed feedback ranging from strong praise to complaints about errors and superficial agreement [E7][E9][E1][E3][E8].

ranking.summary: OpenAI was viewed negatively, with comments calling GPT-4o “notoriously sycophantic” and dismissing the models as inferior in style and creative writing [E9][E7].

Anthropic landed in mixed territory with high-volume, as users praised Claude’s helpfulness and reverse-engineering ability but also reported serious mistakes, weak reasoning in some tasks, and frustratingly superficial agreement [E1][E5][E6][E3][E4][E8][E7].

evidence.excerpt: I do think that the LLMs have gotten much better at this, especially Claude, and will often push back on bad choices.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly praises Claude for improved behavior and for pushing back on bad user choices.

evidence.excerpt: I've found that Opus 4.6 is worse about this than 4.5. 4.5 does a better job IMO of following instructions and not drifting into the mode where it acts like everything i say is a grand revelation from up high.

annotations.rationale: The commenter criticizes Opus 4.6 while praising Opus 4.5, producing a mixed overall assessment of Anthropic's Claude models.

evidence.excerpt: I've been using claude code daily for months and the worst thing that happened wasnt a wipe(yet). It needed to save an svg file so it created a /public/blog/ folder. Which meant Apache started serving that real directory instead of routing /blog. My blog just 404'd and I spent like an hour debugging before I figured it out.

annotations.rationale: The commenter reports Claude Code caused a disruptive mistake that broke their blog routing, a clear negative quality judgment.

evidence.excerpt: I've noticed Claude Code is happy to decompile APKs for you but isn't very good at doing reachability analysis or figuring out complex control flows. It will treat completely dead code as important as a commonly invoked function.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says Claude Code performs poorly on reachability and control-flow analysis.

evidence.excerpt: I've found that Claude Code works well at reversing java applications. Even if it is fully obfuscated claude can restore sensible names for everything and understand how it all works and answer questions about what it is doing.

annotations.rationale: The commenter directly endorses Claude Code's reverse-engineering ability and understanding of obfuscated Java applications.

evidence.excerpt: Combine that with Claude Code and you can build some incredible things. Myself as an example, I used Claude to advise me on starting a business and building a MVP. After a few weeks of refinement I was able to create something I never could have done without Claude. It is a game changer for sure.

annotations.rationale: The commenter gives strong personal praise, saying Claude enabled something they could not have done otherwise and calling it a game changer.

evidence.excerpt: The style isn’t a limit of the technology, it’s a limit of the lobotomized models from OpenAI and Anthropic. The open source community has lots of models that are great at creative writing.

annotations.rationale: The commenter harshly criticizes OpenAI's models as 'lobotomized' and inferior for creative writing.
The commenter harshly criticizes Anthropic's models as 'lobotomized' and inferior for creative writing.

evidence.excerpt: Using Opus 4.6 for research code assistance in physics/chemistry, I've also found that, in situations where I know I'm right, and I know it has gone down a line of incorrect reasoning and assumptions, it will respond to my corrections by pointing out that I'm obviously right, but if enough of the mistakes are in the context, it will then flip back to working based on them: the exclamations of my being right are just superficial. This is not enormously surprising, based on how LLMs work, but is frustrating.

annotations.rationale: The commenter describes Opus 4.6 as superficially agreeable and frustrating when correcting errors, a clear negative assessment.
The commenter directly characterizes GPT-4o as 'notoriously sycophantic,' a strong criticism of the model.
